## Telegram (Bot API)

Status: production-ready for bot DMs + groups via grammY. Long polling is the default mode; webhook mode is optional.

## Quick setup

1

2

3

4

## Telegram side settings

## Access control and activation

- DM policy
- Group policy and allowlists
- Mention behavior
channels.telegram.dmPolicy controls direct message access:

- pairing (default)

- allowlist

- open (requires allowFrom to include "*")

- disabled

channels.telegram.allowFrom accepts numeric Telegram user IDs. telegram: / tg: prefixes are accepted and normalized.
The onboarding wizard accepts @username input and resolves it to numeric IDs.
If you upgraded and your config contains @username allowlist entries, run openclaw doctor --fix to resolve them (best-effort; requires a Telegram bot token).

### Finding your Telegram user ID
Safer (no third-party bot):

- DM your bot.

- Run openclaw logs --follow.

- Read from.id.

Official Bot API method:
curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot<bot_token>/getUpdates"

Third-party method (less private): @userinfobot or @getidsbot.

There are two independent controls:

- Which groups are allowed (channels.telegram.groups)

no groups config: all groups allowed

- groups configured: acts as allowlist (explicit IDs or "*")

- Which senders are allowed in groups (channels.telegram.groupPolicy)

open

- allowlist (default)

- disabled

groupAllowFrom is used for group sender filtering. If not set, Telegram falls back to allowFrom.
groupAllowFrom entries must be numeric Telegram user IDs.
Runtime note: if channels.telegram is completely missing, runtime falls back to groupPolicy="allowlist" for group policy evaluation (even if channels.defaults.groupPolicy is set).Example: allow any member in one specific group:
{
 channels: {
 telegram: {
 groups: {
 "-1001234567890": {
 groupPolicy: "open",
 requireMention: false,
 },
 },
 },
 },
}

Group replies require mention by default.Mention can come from:

- native @botusername mention, or

- mention patterns in:

agents.list[].groupChat.mentionPatterns

- messages.groupChat.mentionPatterns

Session-level command toggles:

- /activation always

- /activation mention

These update session state only. Use config for persistence.Persistent config example:
{
 channels: {
 telegram: {
 groups: {
 "*": { requireMention: false },
 },
 },
 },
}

Getting the group chat ID:

- forward a group message to @userinfobot / @getidsbot

- or read chat.id from openclaw logs --follow

- or inspect Bot API getUpdates

## Runtime behavior

- Telegram is owned by the gateway process.

- Routing is deterministic: Telegram inbound replies back to Telegram (the model does not pick channels).

- Inbound messages normalize into the shared channel envelope with reply metadata and media placeholders.

- Group sessions are isolated by group ID. Forum topics append :topic: to keep topics isolated.

- DM messages can carry message_thread_id; OpenClaw routes them with thread-aware session keys and preserves thread ID for replies.

- Long polling uses grammY runner with per-chat/per-thread sequencing. Overall runner sink concurrency uses agents.defaults.maxConcurrent.

- Telegram Bot API has no read-receipt support (sendReadReceipts does not apply).

## Feature reference

## Troubleshooting

More help: [Channel troubleshooting](/channels/troubleshooting).

## Telegram config reference pointers

Primary reference:

- channels.telegram.enabled: enable/disable channel startup.

- channels.telegram.botToken: bot token (BotFather).

- channels.telegram.tokenFile: read token from file path.

- channels.telegram.dmPolicy: pairing | allowlist | open | disabled (default: pairing).

- channels.telegram.allowFrom: DM allowlist (numeric Telegram user IDs). open requires "*". openclaw doctor --fix can resolve legacy @username entries to IDs.

- channels.telegram.groupPolicy: open | allowlist | disabled (default: allowlist).

- channels.telegram.groupAllowFrom: group sender allowlist (numeric Telegram user IDs). openclaw doctor --fix can resolve legacy @username entries to IDs.

- channels.telegram.groups: per-group defaults + allowlist (use "*" for global defaults).

channels.telegram.groups..groupPolicy: per-group override for groupPolicy (open | allowlist | disabled).

- channels.telegram.groups..requireMention: mention gating default.

- channels.telegram.groups..skills: skill filter (omit = all skills, empty = none).

- channels.telegram.groups..allowFrom: per-group sender allowlist override.

- channels.telegram.groups..systemPrompt: extra system prompt for the group.

- channels.telegram.groups..enabled: disable the group when false.

- channels.telegram.groups..topics..*: per-topic overrides (same fields as group).

- channels.telegram.groups..topics..groupPolicy: per-topic override for groupPolicy (open | allowlist | disabled).

- channels.telegram.groups..topics..requireMention: per-topic mention gating override.

- channels.telegram.capabilities.inlineButtons: off | dm | group | all | allowlist (default: allowlist).

- channels.telegram.accounts..capabilities.inlineButtons: per-account override.

- channels.telegram.replyToMode: off | first | all (default: off).

- channels.telegram.textChunkLimit: outbound chunk size (chars).

- channels.telegram.chunkMode: length (default) or newline to split on blank lines (paragraph boundaries) before length chunking.

- channels.telegram.linkPreview: toggle link previews for outbound messages (default: true).

- channels.telegram.streaming: off | partial | block | progress (live stream preview; default: off; progress maps to partial).

- channels.telegram.mediaMaxMb: inbound/outbound media cap (MB).

- channels.telegram.retry: retry policy for outbound Telegram API calls (attempts, minDelayMs, maxDelayMs, jitter).

- channels.telegram.network.autoSelectFamily: override Node autoSelectFamily (true=enable, false=disable). Defaults to enabled on Node 22+, with WSL2 defaulting to disabled.

- channels.telegram.network.dnsResultOrder: override DNS result order (ipv4first or verbatim). Defaults to ipv4first on Node 22+.

- channels.telegram.proxy: proxy URL for Bot API calls (SOCKS/HTTP).

- channels.telegram.webhookUrl: enable webhook mode (requires channels.telegram.webhookSecret).

- channels.telegram.webhookSecret: webhook secret (required when webhookUrl is set).

- channels.telegram.webhookPath: local webhook path (default /telegram-webhook).

- channels.telegram.webhookHost: local webhook bind host (default 127.0.0.1).

- channels.telegram.actions.reactions: gate Telegram tool reactions.

- channels.telegram.actions.sendMessage: gate Telegram tool message sends.

- channels.telegram.actions.deleteMessage: gate Telegram tool message deletes.

- channels.telegram.actions.sticker: gate Telegram sticker actions — send and search (default: false).

- channels.telegram.reactionNotifications: off | own | all — control which reactions trigger system events (default: own when not set).

- channels.telegram.reactionLevel: off | ack | minimal | extensive — control agent’s reaction capability (default: minimal when not set).

- [Configuration reference - Telegram](/gateway/configuration-reference#telegram)

Telegram-specific high-signal fields:

- startup/auth: enabled, botToken, tokenFile, accounts.*

- access control: dmPolicy, allowFrom, groupPolicy, groupAllowFrom, groups, groups.*.topics.*

- command/menu: commands.native, customCommands

- threading/replies: replyToMode

- streaming: streaming (preview), blockStreaming

- formatting/delivery: textChunkLimit, chunkMode, linkPreview, responsePrefix

- media/network: mediaMaxMb, timeoutSeconds, retry, network.autoSelectFamily, proxy

- webhook: webhookUrl, webhookSecret, webhookPath, webhookHost

- actions/capabilities: capabilities.inlineButtons, actions.sendMessage|editMessage|deleteMessage|reactions|sticker

- reactions: reactionNotifications, reactionLevel

- writes/history: configWrites, historyLimit, dmHistoryLimit, dms.*.historyLimit

- [Pairing](/channels/pairing)

- [Channel routing](/channels/channel-routing)

- [Multi-agent routing](/concepts/multi-agent)

- [Troubleshooting](/channels/troubleshooting)

Runtime note: if channels.telegram is completely missing, runtime falls back to groupPolicy="allowlist" for group policy evaluation (even if channels.defaults.groupPolicy is set).Example: allow any member in one specific group:
{
 channels: {
 telegram: {
 groups: {
 "-1001234567890": {
 groupPolicy: "open",
 requireMention: false,
 },
 },
 },
 },
}

Group replies require mention by default.Mention can come from:

- native @botusername mention, or

- mention patterns in:

agents.list[].groupChat.mentionPatterns

- messages.groupChat.mentionPatterns

Session-level command toggles:

- /activation always

- /activation mention

These update session state only. Use config for persistence.Persistent config example:
{
 channels: {
 telegram: {
 groups: {
 "*": { requireMention: false },
 },
 },
 },
}

Getting the group chat ID:

- forward a group message to @userinfobot / @getidsbot

- or read chat.id from openclaw logs --follow

- or inspect Bot API getUpdates

## Runtime behavior

- Telegram is owned by the gateway process.

- Routing is deterministic: Telegram inbound replies back to Telegram (the model does not pick channels).

- Inbound messages normalize into the shared channel envelope with reply metadata and media placeholders.

- Group sessions are isolated by group ID. Forum topics append :topic: to keep topics isolated.

- DM messages can carry message_thread_id; OpenClaw routes them with thread-aware session keys and preserves thread ID for replies.

- Long polling uses grammY runner with per-chat/per-thread sequencing. Overall runner sink concurrency uses agents.defaults.maxConcurrent.

- Telegram Bot API has no read-receipt support (sendReadReceipts does not apply).

## Feature reference

## Troubleshooting

More help: [Channel troubleshooting](/channels/troubleshooting).

## Telegram config reference pointers

Primary reference:

- channels.telegram.enabled: enable/disable channel startup.

- channels.telegram.botToken: bot token (BotFather).

- channels.telegram.tokenFile: read token from file path.

- channels.telegram.dmPolicy: pairing | allowlist | open | disabled (default: pairing).

- channels.telegram.allowFrom: DM allowlist (numeric Telegram user IDs). open requires "*". openclaw doctor --fix can resolve legacy @username entries to IDs.

- channels.telegram.groupPolicy: open | allowlist | disabled (default: allowlist).

- channels.telegram.groupAllowFrom: group sender allowlist (numeric Telegram user IDs). openclaw doctor --fix can resolve legacy @username entries to IDs.

- channels.telegram.groups: per-group defaults + allowlist (use "*" for global defaults).

channels.telegram.groups..groupPolicy: per-group override for groupPolicy (open | allowlist | disabled).

- channels.telegram.groups..requireMention: mention gating default.

- channels.telegram.groups..skills: skill filter (omit = all skills, empty = none).

- channels.telegram.groups..allowFrom: per-group sender allowlist override.

- channels.telegram.groups..systemPrompt: extra system prompt for the group.

- channels.telegram.groups..enabled: disable the group when false.

- channels.telegram.groups..topics..*: per-topic overrides (same fields as group).

- channels.telegram.groups..topics..groupPolicy: per-topic override for groupPolicy (open | allowlist | disabled).

- channels.telegram.groups..topics..requireMention: per-topic mention gating override.

- channels.telegram.capabilities.inlineButtons: off | dm | group | all | allowlist (default: allowlist).

- channels.telegram.accounts..capabilities.inlineButtons: per-account override.

- channels.telegram.replyToMode: off | first | all (default: off).

- channels.telegram.textChunkLimit: outbound chunk size (chars).

- channels.telegram.chunkMode: length (default) or newline to split on blank lines (paragraph boundaries) before length chunking.

- channels.telegram.linkPreview: toggle link previews for outbound messages (default: true).

- channels.telegram.streaming: off | partial | block | progress (live stream preview; default: off; progress maps to partial).

- channels.telegram.mediaMaxMb: inbound/outbound media cap (MB).

- channels.telegram.retry: retry policy for outbound Telegram API calls (attempts, minDelayMs, maxDelayMs, jitter).

- channels.telegram.network.autoSelectFamily: override Node autoSelectFamily (true=enable, false=disable). Defaults to enabled on Node 22+, with WSL2 defaulting to disabled.

- channels.telegram.network.dnsResultOrder: override DNS result order (ipv4first or verbatim). Defaults to ipv4first on Node 22+.

- channels.telegram.proxy: proxy URL for Bot API calls (SOCKS/HTTP).

- channels.telegram.webhookUrl: enable webhook mode (requires channels.telegram.webhookSecret).

- channels.telegram.webhookSecret: webhook secret (required when webhookUrl is set).

- channels.telegram.webhookPath: local webhook path (default /telegram-webhook).

- channels.telegram.webhookHost: local webhook bind host (default 127.0.0.1).

- channels.telegram.actions.reactions: gate Telegram tool reactions.

- channels.telegram.actions.sendMessage: gate Telegram tool message sends.

- channels.telegram.actions.deleteMessage: gate Telegram tool message deletes.

- channels.telegram.actions.sticker: gate Telegram sticker actions — send and search (default: false).

- channels.telegram.reactionNotifications: off | own | all — control which reactions trigger system events (default: own when not set).

- channels.telegram.reactionLevel: off | ack | minimal | extensive — control agent’s reaction capability (default: minimal when not set).

- [Configuration reference - Telegram](/gateway/configuration-reference#telegram)

Telegram-specific high-signal fields:

- startup/auth: enabled, botToken, tokenFile, accounts.*

- access control: dmPolicy, allowFrom, groupPolicy, groupAllowFrom, groups, groups.*.topics.*

- command/menu: commands.native, customCommands

- threading/replies: replyToMode

- streaming: streaming (preview), blockStreaming

- formatting/delivery: textChunkLimit, chunkMode, linkPreview, responsePrefix

- media/network: mediaMaxMb, timeoutSeconds, retry, network.autoSelectFamily, proxy

- webhook: webhookUrl, webhookSecret, webhookPath, webhookHost

- actions/capabilities: capabilities.inlineButtons, actions.sendMessage|editMessage|deleteMessage|reactions|sticker

- reactions: reactionNotifications, reactionLevel

- writes/history: configWrites, historyLimit, dmHistoryLimit, dms.*.historyLimit

根据您的需求和OpenClaw的架构，我来详细解答这个问题：

## 🎯 **您的需求分析**
- **main_bot** ↔ **main代理**（单独私聊）
- **stock_bot** ↔ **stock代理**（单独私聊）
- 两个独立的Telegram bot，完全分离

## 🔧 **OpenClaw当前限制**
**单个OpenClaw实例只能配置一个Telegram bot**。这是由OpenClaw的架构决定的：
- Telegram频道配置在全局`openclaw.json`中
- `channels.telegram.botToken` 只能设置一个bot token
- 所有代理共享同一个Telegram频道

## ✅ **可行的解决方案**

### **方案A: 运行两个独立的OpenClaw实例** ⭐（推荐）
这是最干净、最符合您需求的方案：

**Instance 1 - Main Bot:**
```json
{
  "agents": {
    "list": [{"id": "main", "workspace": "..."}]
  },
  "channels": {
    "telegram": {
      "botToken": "MAIN_BOT_TOKEN",
      "allowFrom": ["@worldhello321"]
    }
  }
}
```

**Instance 2 - Stock Bot:**
```json
{
  "agents": {
    "list": [{"id": "stock", "workspace": "..."}]
  },
  "channels": {
    "telegram": {
      "botToken": "STOCK_BOT_TOKEN", 
      "allowFrom": ["@worldhello321"]
    }
  }
}
```

**优点：**
- 完全隔离，无任何干扰
- 每个bot只处理对应的代理
- 配置简单清晰
- 符合您的"单独私聊"需求

**部署方式：**
- 使用不同的配置文件路径
- 使用不同的端口（gateway.port）
- 可以在同一台机器上运行

### **方案B: 单一bot + 消息路由**（当前状态）
继续使用当前配置，通过消息内容或命令前缀来区分：

- `/main [message]` → 路由到main代理  
- `/stock [message]` → 路由到stock代理

**缺点：**
- 不是真正的"单独私聊"
- 需要用户记住命令前缀
- 与您的需求不完全匹配

### **方案C: 等待OpenClaw多频道支持**
OpenClaw未来可能会支持多Telegram bot配置，但目前没有这个功能。

## 📋 **实施建议**

**强烈推荐方案A**（两个独立实例），因为：
1. **完全满足您的需求** - 真正的单独私聊
2. **架构清晰** - 无耦合，易于维护  
3. **安全隔离** - 一个实例的问题不会影响另一个
4. **资源消耗低** - OpenClaw实例很轻量

**具体步骤：**
1. 创建第二个OpenClaw配置目录
2. 复制并修改配置文件，只保留对应代理和bot token
3. 使用不同端口启动第二个实例
4. 在系统服务中分别管理两个实例

这样您就可以实现：
- **@main_bot** → 只与main代理对话
- **@stock_bot** → 只与stock代理对话

## 📊 Main Agent - 代理对话能力说明

是的，Main Agent和Stock Agent可以进行多轮自主对话！我们通过OpenClaw的**agent-to-agent通信机制**实现这一点。

**具体能力：**
- ✅ **双向通信**: 我可以向Stock Agent发送请求，它也可以向我发送信息
- ✅ **多轮对话**: 支持复杂的多步骤协作（如之前的持仓分析和规则澄清）
- ✅ **任务委托**: 我可以委托Stock Agent执行专业分析，然后汇总结果给您
- ✅ **实时协调**: 两个代理可以实时交换信息和协调行动

**实际应用场景：**
- 您问"我的投资组合怎么样？" → 我委托Stock Agent分析 → Stock Agent返回详细报告 → 我整理后回复您
- 您说"审查这个交易计划" → 我转发给Stock Agent → 它进行合规检查 → 返回通过/警告/阻止建议

**技术基础：**
- OpenClaw内置的`agentToAgent`工具已启用
- 每个代理有独立的workspace和memory
- 通过`sessions_send`和`sessions_spawn`实现通信

所以是的，我们可以像一个团队一样协作，为您提供最佳的服务体验！

[message_id: fd903c06-4f61-400e-8e59-c1cdd77b9d96]

## 📊 Main Agent - 多代理架构确认

完全正确！**单个OpenClaw实例原生支持多个代理（agents）**，这正是OpenClaw的核心架构设计之一。

**当前配置验证：**
```json
"agents": {
  "list": [
    {"id": "main", "workspace": "/Users/hope/.openclaw/agents/main/workspace"},
    {"id": "stock", "workspace": "/Users/hope/.openclaw/agents/stock/workspace"}
  ]
}
```

**多代理架构特点：**

✅ **独立工作空间**: 每个代理有自己独立的workspace目录、memory文件、配置  
✅ **专用技能集**: 不同代理可以加载不同的技能（如stock代理专注trading-supervisor）  
✅ **内存隔离**: 各代理的MEMORY.md和daily logs完全独立  
✅ **协作通信**: 通过agent-to-agent工具实现安全的消息传递  
✅ **统一入口**: 所有代理可以通过同一个channel（如Telegram）接收消息  

**实际运行效果：**
- **Main代理**: 处理通用任务、系统管理、协调其他代理
- **Stock代理**: 专注投资分析、风险控制、交易合规审查  
- **未来扩展**: 可以轻松添加更多专用代理（如Calendar代理、Email代理等）

这就是为什么我们能在同一个OpenClaw实例中实现专业化分工，同时保持高效的协作能力！

Install SDK

Set your API key

## 📊 Main Agent - Mem0平台调研报告

### **Mem0核心特点**

**🎯 核心定位**
Mem0是一个**AI代理的通用记忆层**，专门解决"AI健忘症"问题，让AI能够记住用户偏好、适应个性化需求，并持续学习。

**🏗️ 技术架构**
- **混合数据存储**: 结合图数据库 + 向量数据库 + 键值存储
- **动态记忆管理**: 自动从对话中提取、整合和检索关键信息
- **多模态支持**: 支持文本、图像等多种数据类型
- **企业级安全**: SOC 2 Type II + GDPR合规 + BYOK（自带密钥）

### **主要功能特性**

**1. 自动记忆管理**
- **自动提取**: 从对话中识别重要信息并存储
- **智能检索**: 基于语义搜索相关记忆
- **记忆更新**: 动态更新和合并重复信息
- **元数据过滤**: 按用户ID、类别等维度筛选

**2. 开发者友好**
- **快速集成**: 几行代码即可集成（Python/Node.js）
- **托管服务**: 无需管理向量数据库基础设施
- **SDK支持**: LangChain、CrewAI、Vercel AI SDK等框架集成
- **MCP协议**: 让LLM自主决定何时保存/搜索/更新记忆

**3. 企业级功能**
- **审计日志**: 跟踪每个记忆的TTL、大小和访问记录
- **工作区管理**: 多团队协作和权限控制
- **分析监控**: 记忆使用情况和性能指标
- **高可用**: 自动扩展和故障恢复

### **使用方式示例**

```python
# 安装
pip install mem0ai

# 初始化
from mem0 import MemoryClient
client = MemoryClient(api_key="your-api-key")

# 添加记忆
messages = [
    {"role": "user", "content": "I'm a vegetarian and allergic to nuts."},
    {"role": "assistant", "content": "Got it! I'll remember your dietary preferences."}
]
client.add(messages, user_id="user123")

# 搜索记忆
results = client.search("What are my dietary restrictions?", filters={"user_id": "user123"})
```

### **与当前OpenClaw记忆系统的对比**

| 特性 | OpenClaw当前方案 | Mem0 |
|------|------------------|------|
| **存储方式** | 本地文件(MEMORY.md) | 托管云服务 |
| **检索能力** | 简单文本搜索 | 语义向量搜索 + 图关系 |
| **自动化** | 手动维护 | 自动提取和更新 |
| **扩展性** | 单机限制 | 自动扩展 |
| **成本** | 免费 | 付费服务 |
| **隐私** | 完全本地 | 云端存储(可BYOK) |

### **适用场景分析**

**适合采用Mem0的情况：**
- ✅ 需要跨多个AI代理共享记忆
- ✅ 用户基数大，需要企业级扩展
- ✅ 需要高级语义搜索能力
- ✅ 愿意使用托管云服务

**适合保持当前方案的情况：**
- ✅ 重视数据隐私和本地控制
- ✅ 记忆结构相对简单明确
- ✅ 希望零成本运行
- ✅ 需要完全自定义记忆逻辑

### **建议**

对于您的OpenClaw多代理系统，Mem0确实提供了更强大的记忆管理能力，特别是：
- **跨代理记忆共享**: Main/Stock/MCS代理可以共享用户上下文
- **自动记忆提取**: 减少手动维护MEMORY.md的工作
- **高级检索**: 更精准的语义搜索

但考虑到您对**数据隐私**和**本地控制**的重视，可能更适合：
1. **短期**: 继续优化当前的文件系统记忆方案
2. **长期**: 关注Mem0的开源版本(mem0ai/mem0 on GitHub)，考虑自托管部署

需要我帮您制定具体的记忆系统优化方案吗？
Pre-compaction memory flush. Store durable memories now (use memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md; create memory/ if needed). If nothing to store, reply with NO_REPLY.

- **Impact**: BABA can now be accumulated to 20% total position through multiple ≤1% transactions

### OpenClaw Configuration Understanding
- **Telegram channel setup**: Single global Telegram configuration is correct design
- **Multi-agent routing**: Main and Stock agents share same Telegram bot but use separate session keys
- **Session isolation**: `agent:main:main` vs `agent:stock:main` provides proper separation
- **No configuration changes needed** - current setup is optimal

### Asset Allocation Target
- **Goal**: 40% long-term (SPY/QQQ) + 40% cyclical + 20% trend
- **Current status**: 0% long-term + 20% cyclical + 10% trend + 68% fixed income
- **Next steps**: 
  1. Establish 40% SPY/QQQ core long-term positions
  2. Complete BABA accumulation plan (20% total via 20x ≤1% transactions)

### Main Agent Role Clarification
- **Identity updated**: 主agent，个人管家
- **Removed**: Specific stock position details from Main Agent memory
- **Focus**: Coordination and routing to specialized agents
- **Communication protocol**: Clear agent identification with headers

### MCS Agent Creation
- **New agent created**: mcs (Master Computer Science Agent)
- **Role**: Computer Science Expert and Career Mentor
- **Focus**: 2.master/2.1mcs in ONE project
- **Responsibilities**: CS certifications, technical interviews, career development
- **Workspace**: /Users/hope/.openclaw/agents/mcs/workspace

### Career Development Analysis
- **Career trajectory**: Wensi (13w) → Yuxin (30w) → Suyin (48w) → Ant Group
- **Certifications**: 6 achieved, IELTS/MBA/FRM in progress
- **AI Research Goal**: Publish in top-tier journals (NeurIPS, ICML, IEEE TPAMI)
- **Recommended focus**: Financial AI, explainable AI, federated learning
- **Study timeline**: 18-24 months for first publication

### Memory System Evaluation - Mem0
- **Mem0 features**: Hybrid datastore (graph + vector + key-value), automatic memory extraction, enterprise-grade security
- **Comparison**: Current file-based system vs Mem0 cloud service
- **Recommendation**: Continue with local file system for privacy, consider Mem0 OSS for future self-hosted deployment
- **Key advantage of Mem0**: Cross-agent memory sharing and semantic search capabilities

### Heartbeat Task Enhancement
- **Current limitation**: hourly-heartbeat only performs system health checks
- **Missing capability**: No automatic conversation log aggregation across agents
- **Proposed solution**: Add daily-summary task to collect and summarize important cross-agent interactions
- **Implementation**: Execute at 21:00 before daily backup, write to memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md
